title: I fixed my sleep by swimming
author: u/Final_Solid_617
body: I always felt like my body was so tired during my insomnia but my mind was wide awake. Turns out: yes — my body was tired and my mind was awake, but my body also was not tired in the right way. I’ve recently been on a rigorous swimming routine (5x a week) and let me tell you… exercising like this helps me sleep like a BABY. My sleep lasts 9 beautiful hours and even if I wake up during the night, I fall asleep so quickly again. I never was fond of exercise or any type of sport, but I realized my body got tired from the sedentary routine I was living. I was getting my steps in and biked daily, but my shoulders slouch at my desk, my back would be aching, my mind would be restless and it would feel like my circulation just was not activated in my whole body. When I swim (even for half an hour) I come home kinda drowsy and sleepy, and I’m excited to lay my head down. Idk — maybe silly advice, but it might help someone here!

title: Balancing sleep and graveyard shift
author: u/-srirachasauce-
body: I work from 11pm-7am five days a week. I’ve been working this shift for six months now and I still haven’t figured out a way to commit to a consistent sleep schedule. I feel like most of my day is dedicated to just sleeping and working, and I miss having more time for myself. How do some of you do it? Is there any tricks or advice that I’m missing?

title: I’m terrified of going to sleep
author: u/Correct-Sound-2225
body: They keep giving me ideas like “keep your phone away, white noise , wind down… etc” but if i don’t have my phone i literally can’t stand my brain nor the feelings. My mom used to force me to sleep she would literally sit by the door so i do move out of bed i also would have scary thoughts like monsters crawling on me i would sleep so scared lately i’ve wondering if my sleep problems isn’t caused by me having less self control or bad habits but more of deep trauma that still hunts me

title: Is there a scientific reason why I am WIDE AWAKE in times I want to sleep?!
author: u/Rough_Journey
body: I was dead tired at work. I was slumping in my chair. No work was done. I almost swerved into the oncoming traffic. But the moment I got home and climbed into bed suddenly my body was like "fuxk you. Awake." Like I want to sleep. I don't want to stay up all night again. Just give me 6hrs instead of 3 please...

title: How I stopped doomscrolling at night and actually slept early
author: u/pastryzig
body: Like your a teen who has a curfew, put your gadgets in a different room quite far from yours. I recently stopped doing this but ever since I stopped, I started going to bed in the early AM's instead of what when I normally fell asleep when I separated myself from my devices which was around 10-10:30pm or earlier sometimes. I wake up early on my own without any alarms (ranging from 6am to 8am) and I actually wake up feeling rested. If you start having a lot of thoughts, ideas, or questions, write them down on a piece of paper by your bedside and check back on them when you wake up. Don't get your phone to write these down or answer those questions. This helps stop my mind from racing because I got them out of my head and can just deal with them the next morning. Just a reminder that this is advice based on my experience these last 4 months when I started this.

title: How do people sleep so late then still wake up early like they're not lacking sleep?
author: u/Infamous_Context_244

title: I am always tired after waking up
author: u/r0selyn
body: My dreams would be entertaining to the people that find dreams entertaining, but i am tired of them, annoyed by them, my mind would make sudden flashbacks to the dreams ive had last night, the night before or even a year ago, i wake up tired, i never sleep well, it feels like my brain has been working all night, i have most if not all apd symptoms and i dont know whether theyre caused by this bad quality of sleep or if i really do have apd. Im tired and i dont know what to do, ive been taking magnesium glycinate, forgetting at times to drink it 1-2 hours before sleeping but it hasnt helped and its been over 2 months since i started taking it, what should i do?

title: nightly routine
author: u/yezihan1017
body: I am made of 3 things: "I'll sleep early tonight," doomscrolling, and the consequences of my own actions.

title: My sleep got noticeably better after adding one small thing before bed
author: u/venzzzleep
body: I’ve been struggling with sleep for a while. Falling asleep was hit or miss, and even when I did, I’d still wake up in the middle of the night. Tried the usual stuff like melatonin, magnesium, routines… helped a bit but nothing consistent. Recently I started taking something before bed, just a few drops, and my sleep actually feels different now. I fall asleep easier and don’t wake up as much. It’s not like I get knocked out, it just feels like my body actually relaxes. Didn’t expect something that simple to make this much difference honestly. Anyone else have something small like that work for them?

title: does anyone else overthink so much they can’t sleep?
author: u/laaaayyyy
body: this might sound dumb but my brain literally won’t shut up at night like during the day i’m tired as hell, low energy, can’t even focus properly… but the second i lay down it’s like my brain wakes up i start thinking about everything. stress, random memories, stuff i need to do, even stupid conversations from years ago and it just keeps going even when i sleep it doesn’t feel deep? like i wake up still tired lowkey starting to affect my days a lot is this normal or what… how do you even fix this

title: Really tired of sleeping 5 hours only ever night
author: u/Disastrous-Corner957
body: Im 23 M and have in this situation for 2 years. I sleep at midnight and usually wake up between 4:30 to 5 am extremely tired. Despite waking up tired i can't go back to sleep until 7 am. I have no problem falling asleep but staying asleep is. Im tired and dysfonctionnal during the day and this is really ruinning my studies,relationships and daily life so bad. Sometimes it gets so bad i become so depressed and just stay in the house and do nothing. Any help guys please.

title: Technical and focused way to fall asleep?
author: u/thehustlerofi
body: I'm looking for a very technical way to fall asleep. I mean some strict and precise steps that go through my head while I'm at the moment of trying to fall asleep. I want this because witout it i feel kind of confused and alert (because not falling asleep consecnses are very bad for me).like for example i may think:"wait,am i focusing on my breathing to much?" Or "am i watching myself falling asleep right now?" I prefer a clear method I can follow every time like a clock,without hesitation,and with deep focus that can be achieved through practice . I want that method to have very clear steps.like for example,a method that contain clearing mind step is not good,because I might think of many way to clear my mind:focusing on breathing, looking at the dark caused by my eyes closed,whisper in my head shhhhhh sound. So it will confuse me. I need very precise steps. I'm pretty sure that monks sleep good because they have the ability to focuse very hard and that is what I'm trying to achieve here

title: What Works To Help You Sleep?
author: u/EpicureanRd
body: I've been making binaural sleep music for years (and music with underlying frequencies that promote better sleep) and finally organized it into a free playlist. I'm also working on creating nature sounds with underlying frequencies to promote sleep, as well. I used to have a lot of sleep troubles, but using my tracks seems to help me sleep better and deeper (finally getting 80's to 90's on my sleep monitors). So far, I've just posted my very basic sounds. But in a few days to weeks, I'm going to be posting classical music, then nature sounds, all with the underlying sleep frequencies on youtube so that people can listen at no charge. Anyone else use frequency-based music or sounds for sleep? Curious what's worked for you. I'm probably going to add to the collection based on recommendations here.

title: Need help with sleep
author: u/Spare_Butterfly_1256
body: I'm 26 and I've had trouble sleeping for half my life. sometimes I'll sleep really well, and it might last for a couple days, but then I'm back to having trouble sleeping. Yesterday was the worst, where after a night where I only got maybe 1-2 hours of sleep and got up at 2pm, I went to bed at 10pm and had insomnia again where I was only able to fall asleep sometime after 9am. I took way too many melatonin gummies in my desperation to get any sleep at all. It might've helped because I did manage to fall asleep eventually, even if it wasn't for as long as I would've liked and I kept waking up and falling asleep again, but I'm scared what my sleep's gonna be like tonight. I've never taken any sleep pills, but I'm thinking of it now. I usually use CBN, THC, and/or melatonin gummies to help me sleep, but they just don't work sometimes, like last night. I was desperate enough that I was looking up ways to safely make myself pass out, and thinking of getting some alcohol even though I don't drink. I get anxiety about being able to sleep, which certainly doesn't help, and my sleep schedule's a mess due to the aforementioned insomnia. I don't have insurance so I don't know how expensive it would be to get a consultation. I just don't know what to do. Does anyone have any advice?

title: How do I wake up early when I can’t fall asleep until 1-2 am?
author: u/Huge-Elevator-7541
body: How do I wake up early when I can’t fall asleep until 1-2 am?

title: I used microdosing of a mostly unheard of mushroom to cure 20 years of chronic insomnia
author: u/Theamanitawarrior
body: Hi all, I had chronic insomnia for about twenty years caused by a combination of childhood trauma, PTSD and very bad medical advice which left me chronically unable to sleep for most of my life. Obviously this made it very hard for me to function so I wasn't able to find a real solution for a long time. Without telling my whole life story, at the age of 33, I discovered a plethora of red and white mushrooms growing in a forest and decided to collect them and try them. After a lot of mistakes in preparation I eventually got the preparation formula correct and from that day forward I never had difficulty sleeping ever again. The Mushroom I found is called Amanita Muscaria. This mushroom has a lot of lore and mystery around it. It is associated with Christmas, Mario, and even Jesus. But it is generslly misunderstood because if it is consumed raw it can be deadly. Preparing it requires cooking it at low ph, so with some kind of acid such as lemon or vitamin C. Due to this misunderstanding in preparation, Amanita is not generally considered to be a sleep aid by the public. My life story says otherwise. It took about 3 years of taking it before I could sleep regularly without using it. However I dont think this would normally be the case since I was chronically sleep deprived for basically my whole adult life. I now forage this mushroom and have a microdosing business and do what I can to share this life-saver with the world. I have had multiple customers who measured up to a %500 increase in REM sleep as a result of microdosing Amanita. It measurably improves sleep. I understand a lot of people get upset when this is discussed, as if modern medicine has a solution, when it really doesn't. There is nothing worse in my opinion than being chronically sleep deprived and I don't think anyone needs to live through that anymore. Alternative solutions work best for some people. I wanted to simply leave this note here for anyone that might be interested in trying it or researching it more for themself without pushing my ideas too much here. If anyone wants more information feel free to ask.

title: Anyone tried hydroxyzine for sleep?
author: u/Top_Effect5135
body: Did it work for you? I’ve heard lots of people say they use this and it’s the best otc sleep aid. Would you recommend it?

body: r/sleep - Magnesium for Sleep quality anyone?

title: I finally found something that helped me fall asleep faster (not instantly, but it works)
author: u/Direct_Review218
body: I used to lie in bed for hours trying to sleep and getting more frustrated. The more I tried, the more awake I felt. What helped me a bit was stopping the “forcing sleep” part and just calming my body first… I’d do some gentle stretching, sit quietly breathing for a few minutes with no phone, and sometimes play a short yoga nidra. It didn’t knock me out instantly, but I fall asleep way easier now. Please let me know if you have any questions ♥️

title: I'm tired and I don't know how to finally sleep well
author: u/REMIZERexe
body: 15M here, I'm extremely jealous of my friend who after always having a poor sleep like me managed to go sleep at a set time and get up WITHOUT ALARM every morning at 6 and feel good. I'm fuckin done. I'm getting up and feeling like a sack of flesh without muscles, I don't even want to think about anything for about 3 hours. Then I KINDA feel more energetic and then noon/afternoon I fall into a pit. I can just fall on the couch and I will not get up until evening sometimes. This fucks up so much, my mental health, my preparation for exams, I don't get ANYTHING DONE IT'S JUST TRASH I'M TRASH ALL DAY
